rationale_for_baseline_tier: The patient has had exertional near-syncope with a rapid-palpitation (arrhythmic) prodrome, a two-month worsening pattern, and a family history of premature sudden cardiac death — a do-not-miss inherited-cardiomyopathy / malignant-arrhythmia picture. He is currently haemodynamically stable and asymptomatic at rest, so an immediate 000 ambulance is not mandated at this moment; but the confirming tests (ECG, Holter, cardiac MRI) and examination cannot be done in telehealth, and exertional syncope with a heritable SCD risk warrants same-day in-person cardiac evaluation with an ECG. T4 (same-day ED / urgent cardiac assessment, self-transport with parents who are present) is the honest tier. Any recurrence at rest, actual syncope, or chest pain escalates immediately to T5 (call 000). A minimum viable pass is T3 (urgent same-day in-person assessment); anything at or below T2 is critical under-triage for this presentation.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Palpitations recur while at rest (not during exertion)
escalation_edges.rationale: Arrhythmia at rest signals a sustained/uncontrolled ventricular arrhythmia and imminent risk of collapse — emergency ambulance response is required.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your heart starts racing again while you are just sitting or resting, call 000 for an ambulance straight away and tell them you have a suspected heart rhythm problem.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: An actual fainting/syncopal episode (loss of consciousness)
escalation_edges.rationale: True syncope in this context may represent a life-threatening ventricular arrhythmia; call 000, and if the patient stops breathing or is unresponsive, begin CPR.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you actually pass out, your parents must call 000 immediately. If you are unresponsive and not breathing normally, they should start CPR and stay on the line with the ambulance operator.

escalation_edges.rationale: New severe chest pain in a young patient with a suspected cardiomyopathy warrants emergency evaluation for an acute cardiac event.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get severe chest pain, call 000 immediately — do not wait to see if it passes.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Baseline: exertional syncope with family history of sudden death, currently stable at rest
escalation_edges.rationale: Even while stable, the constellation mandates same-day in-person cardiac assessment with an ECG; it must not be deferred to a routine appointment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Because you fainted during exercise and a relative died suddenly young, you need to be seen in person today for an urgent heart check and an ECG — please have your parents take you to the emergency department or an urgent cardiac assessment now, and do not exert yourself on the way.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Young athlete for whom exercise is central to identity and routine
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Anticipate reluctance to stop training; be firm that complete exercise cessation is non-negotiable until cardiology clears him, and explain that this is precisely the population in which exertion triggers sudden cardiac death.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Parents are present and are the ones who would call 000 and perform CPR if needed
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Direct the safety-netting and CPR instructions to the parents explicitly, since the patient could lose consciousness; confirm they know how and when to call 000.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: You need an urgent in-person heart assessment today — please have your parents take you to the emergency department (or an urgent cardiac service that can do an ECG today). Until then, stop all exercise completely and rest; only walk gently around the house. Avoid caffeine and stimulants. If your heart races again at rest, if you actually faint, or if you get severe chest pain, call 000 for an ambulance immediately.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Same-day in-person: emergency department or urgent cardiology with immediate ECG; call 000 for any recurrence at rest, syncope or chest pain.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: If it is after hours, still go to the emergency department today rather than waiting for a GP appointment; 000 is available 24 hours for any recurrence at rest, fainting or chest pain.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Provide the GP/cardiologist with the exertional syncope history, the arrhythmic prodrome, the two-month worsening course, and the family history of premature sudden cardiac death, so the urgent ECG, Holter, echocardiogram and cardiac MRI and family-screening pathway can be arranged.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating exertional syncope with an arrhythmic prodrome and a family history of premature sudden death as a benign faint or anxiety, deferring to a routine appointment or self-care.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: The patient continues or resumes exercise and suffers a fatal ventricular arrhythmia / sudden cardiac death before the inherited cardiomyopathy is ever assessed.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Same-day assessment is arranged but framed as merely prompt rather than urgent, or the strict exercise-cessation and 000 recurrence safety-net are underplayed.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Dispatching an emergency ambulance for a patient who is currently stable and asymptomatic at rest, rather than same-day self-transport to in-person assessment — defensible caution, but not the most proportionate response absent recurrence.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: The exact diagnosis (ARVC vs HCM vs a channelopathy) cannot be determined remotely, and the patient looks well at rest.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Name the danger pattern rather than the exact label, act on the highest-risk possibility, and route to urgent same-day in-person cardiac assessment with clear recurrence safety-netting.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Using diagnostic uncertainty or a well resting appearance as a reason to reassure, defer, or clear the patient to exercise.
